skill.name: database
skill.description: Database development and operations workflow covering SQL, NoSQL, database design, migrations, optimization, and data engineering.
skill.summary: Comprehensive workflow for database design, development, optimization, migrations, and data engineering with SQL and NoSQL support.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 69 patterns (53 external_commands, 16 cryptographic) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The detected 'backtick execution' patterns are Markdown code block delimiters containing skill references, not shell commands. No cryptographic code exists in this documentation-only file. This skill is purely informational content describing database workflows.

content.value_statement: Streamline database development with a structured workflow covering design, migrations, optimization, and data pipelines. Eliminate ad-hoc approaches with proven phases for SQL and NoSQL systems.
actual_capabilities: Guide through 7-phase database workflow from design to operations
Reference specialized skills for PostgreSQL, NoSQL, and ORM setup
Provide copy-paste prompts for each workflow phase
Cover data pipeline development with Airflow and dbt
Include data quality and backup automation workflows
Support multiple database technologies: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, ClickHouse
limitations: This is a workflow bundle that references other skills, not a standalone tool
Requires access to referenced sub-skills for full functionality
Does not execute database operations directly
Focused on workflow guidance rather than hands-on database management

use_cases.title: Database Schema Design Project
use_cases.description: Follow the structured 7-phase workflow to design, implement, and optimize a new database schema with proper migrations and quality gates.

use_cases.title: Query Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Use Phase 3 optimization workflow to analyze slow queries, review execution plans, and implement indexing strategies.

use_cases.title: Data Pipeline Implementation
use_cases.description: Leverage Phase 5 to design ETL pipelines with Airflow DAGs and dbt transformations for data warehouse integration.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Start Database Design
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to design a database for a new e-commerce application. Walk me through Phase 1 of the database workflow and help me gather requirements, design the schema, and plan for scalability.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project with database needs

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Optimize Slow Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: My PostgreSQL queries are running slowly. Use Phase 3 optimization workflow to analyze execution plans, suggest index improvements, and refactor inefficient queries.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Plan Database Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to migrate from MySQL to PostgreSQL with zero downtime. Guide me through Phase 4 migration planning, including testing strategy and data integrity verification.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Build Data Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a real-time data pipeline using Airflow and dbt that ingests from multiple sources, transforms data, and loads into our data warehouse. Include monitoring and quality checks.

output_examples.input: Design a user authentication database schema
output_examples.output: A complete schema with users table, sessions table, password reset tokens, proper indexing on email and session_id, and migration scripts for PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM configuration.

output_examples.input: Optimize this slow SELECT query with multiple JOINs
output_examples.output: Query analysis showing missing indexes on foreign keys, recommendation for composite index on (status, created_at), and refactored query using CTEs for better readability and performance.

best_practices: Always design database schemas with future scalability in mind from Phase 1
Test migrations thoroughly in staging before production deployment
Implement monitoring and alerting as part of Phase 7 operations
anti_patterns: Skipping Phase 2 migration testing and deploying directly to production
Optimizing queries without reviewing execution plans first
Implementing data pipelines without data quality validation gates

faq.question: Do I need all referenced sub-skills to use this workflow?
faq.answer: No, you can use relevant phases independently. For example, use only Phase 3 for query optimization without needing data pipeline skills.

faq.question: Which databases does this workflow support?
faq.answer: Primary focus is PostgreSQL, with dedicated sections for MongoDB, Redis, and data warehousing tools like ClickHouse and dbt.

faq.question: Can I adapt this workflow for small projects?
faq.answer: Yes, scale the workflow to your needs. Small projects may only need Phases 1, 2, and 7 for basic design, implementation, and backups.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with existing CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Phase 2 migrations and Phase 7 operations integrate with CI/CD. Use migration testing in CI and backup monitoring in your deployment pipeline.

faq.question: Is this workflow suitable for NoSQL databases?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow includes NoSQL design patterns in Phase 1 and references MongoDB and Redis-specific skills throughout.

faq.question: What quality gates should I implement before production?
faq.answer: Complete all 6 quality gates: schema review, migration testing, performance benchmarks, backup configuration, monitoring setup, and documentation.
